front: Sculpture: concrete cast of the entire interior of a Victorian terraced house; won the 1993 Turner Prize
Sculpture: concrete cast of a house at 193 Grove Road in Mile End; featured graffiti reading "Wot for" and "Why not"
Sculpture: mountains of white polyethylene boxes filling the Tate Modern's Turbine Hall; inspired by a box from the artist's late mother's belongings
Sculpture: Holocaust memorial in Vienna's Judenplatz featuring a library of titleless concrete books with their spines turned inward
Sculpture: inverted transparent resin cast of Trafalgar Square's fourth plinth, placed on top of the plinth itself
Sculpture: plaster cast of an entire room's interior; artist described the process as "mummifying the air inside a room"
Sculpture: Shallow Breath, cast from the underside of a bed
Artist: British sculptor; first female winner of the Turner Prize (1993); known for casts of negative space
Artist: Young British Artist alongside Tracey Emin and Damien Hirst; technique involves plaster and concrete molds of interiors of rooms and everyday objects
